name: Lemminkainen
description: Lemminkainen, also known as Kaukomieli and Ahti, is a prominent figure in Finnish mythology and one of the central heroes of the Kalevala. He is a handsome, reckless, and boastful adventurer, known for his magical abilities, irresistible charm, and insatiable desire for women. Born of a virgin mother, he quickly developed into a skilled warrior and shaman. Lemminkainen's adventures are filled with dangerous quests, romantic escapades, and near-death experiences. He is often depicted as a flawed hero, driven by his passions and prone to making impulsive decisions, which often lead him into trouble. One of his most famous exploits is his attempt to win the hand of the beautiful maiden Kyllikki, which ultimately ends in disappointment and a broken promise. He also undertakes the perilous quest to hunt the Swan of Tuonela, a deadly task that results in his dismemberment and death, only to be resurrected by his mother using her powerful magic and a comb. Lemminkainen represents the untamed spirit of the Finnish wilderness and the complexities of human nature, embodying both admirable courage and reckless impulsiveness. His stories highlight the importance of honor, the consequences of broken promises, and the enduring power of a mother's love.
subtitle: Reckless Hero of the Kalevala

keyMyths.title: Lemminkainen's Wooing of Kyllikki
keyMyths.description: Lemminkainen seeks the hand of Kyllikki, promising to abandon his wandering ways. However, both break their vows, leading to their separation and further adventures for Lemminkainen. The source is the Kalevala.

keyMyths.title: The Hunt for the Swan of Tuonela
keyMyths.description: Lemminkainen attempts to hunt the Swan of Tuonela as a test of his worthiness. He is killed and dismembered, but his mother resurrects him with magic. The source is the Kalevala.

keyMyths.title: Lemminkainen's Boat Building
keyMyths.description: Lemminkainen constructs a magical boat. This act highlights his skill and resourcefulness, showcasing his ability to overcome challenges. The source is the Kalevala.

sources.author: Elias Lönnrot
sources.description: The primary source for Finnish mythology and Lemminkainen's stories.
sources.text: The primary source for Finnish mythology and Lemminkainen's stories.
